text: twenty-five years old male patient with Crohn's disease for three years. He had a history of multiple perianal abscesses and fistulae treated previously by drainage, seton and lift procedure respectively. He was referred to our department with recurrent disease, evaluation was confirmed by MRI, and then examination under anesthesia was performed for draining the abscess and a seton was placed.

The patient is positioned supine under general anesthesia. The surgeon makes two 5mm incisions on both sides of the lower abdomen. A blunt tip cannula is used to inject a mixture of lidocaine, adrenaline, and Ringer's solution into the subcutaneous fat of the lower abdomen.

The surgeon uses a 3.5 mm liposuction cannula and a 50 ml syringe to manually collect approximately 100 ml of Lipoaz Pirate. The collected suspension is transferred to 10 ml syringes for centrifugation. Meanwhile, the patient's incisions are covered with pressure plasters, and the patient is repositioned to a lithotomy position for the next phase.

The surgeon expels the aqueous infranatant fluid from the syringe, leaving only the fat. Using a three-way stopcock and two 10-ml syringes, the adipose tissue is homogenized and softened by repeatedly shifting it back and forth between the syringes, preparing it for injection.

The surgeon curettes the fistula tract through the external opening, removing granulation tissue. A ureteral catheter is tied to the seton, and the internal opening is debrided and curetted for closure. Three pre-taken absorbable sutures are prepared, including the internal sphincter. Two small incisions are made at each side of the external opening, and adipose tissue is injected around the internal opening and fistula tract. The injection is repeated at different levels, creating a firm swelling around the fistula tract.

The surgeon uses a sharp Coleman Cobra head fat injection cannula to transversely cut the fistula tract, releasing fibrotic scar tissue. Adipose tissue is then interposed between the cut ends. Finally, the three pre-taken absorbable sutures at the internal opening are tied.
